name: R-symmetric supersymmetry (MRSSM)
summary: Imposes a continuous U(1)_R (or large discrete) R-symmetry on the SUSY-extended Standard Model. Forbids Majorana gaugino masses, A-terms, and the standard mu-term; gauginos become Dirac fermions (paired with new chiral 'adjoint' superfields). Suppresses SUSY flavor and CP violation by construction, allowing TeV-scale squarks with order-one flavor mixing. Provides a SUSY framework consistent with stringent EDM and FCNC bounds.

addresses_puzzles.note: R-symmetry suppresses flavor mixing in the soft sector (the 'SUSY flavor problem'); allows order-one squark-flavor mixing without K-Kbar / B-Bbar / mu -> e gamma violation, making generic flavor structures compatible with experiment.

touches_benchmarks.note: R-symmetry forbids Majorana phases and A-term phases at leading order, suppressing SUSY EDM contributions; resolves the 'SUSY CP problem'.
Flavor-blind soft masses + R-symmetry suppression keeps mu -> e gamma well below MEG bound.
Dirac gauginos suppress SUSY contributions to K-Kbar / B-Bbar mixing relative to Majorana MSSM.

new_fields: Dirac partners for gauginos (chiral adjoint superfields O, T, S)
second Higgs sector R_u, R_d to pair with H_u, H_d
new_symmetries: continuous U(1)_R or large discrete R-symmetry
introduces.note: Builds on MSSM. New parameters: Dirac-gaugino masses, R-Higgs sector, mu-replacement term, additional Yukawa structure.

history: Hall-Randall (1991) early R-symmetric SUSY. Fox-Nelson-Weiner (2002, hep-ph/0206096) Dirac gauginos. Kribs, Poppitz, Weiner (2007), 'Flavor in supersymmetry with an extended R symmetry', Phys. Rev. D 78, 055010 (2008), arXiv:0712.2039 — modern MRSSM framework. Active LHC phenomenology of Dirac-gaugino signatures (squarks decaying via Dirac higgsino + chiral adjoints).

references.citation: Kribs, Poppitz, Weiner, 'Flavor in supersymmetry with an extended R symmetry', Phys. Rev. D 78, 055010 (2008)
